title: deschampsia cespitosa subsp. wilhelmsii
intro: Deschampsia cespitosa subsp. wilhelmsii, also known as mountain hair grass or Welsh moor grass, is a subspecies of a perennial grass that grows in mountainous regions of Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be beneficial for soothing wounds and reducing swelling. The health benefits of Deschampsia cespitosa subsp. wilhelmsii include its ability to help reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of tinctures, infusions, and salves, which are often used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
